state_description: Coarse cropping/horticulture activity bundle with farm fuel use, irrigation/pumping electricity and process emissions dominated by soils and fertiliser-related nitrous oxide.
output_quantity_basis: One million 2024 Australian dollars of farm-gate output in the named coarse agricultural bundle.
cost_components_summary: Placeholder incremental non-energy supply/abatement cost. Full agricultural production cost is outside Phase 1 scope.
input_basis_notes: Direct on-farm energy inputs only, allocated from official agriculture sector totals using bundle-level assumptions. Input coefficients are coarse and value-normalised.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct on-farm energy and process emissions represented at a coarse bundle level. Major non-CO2 agricultural emissions are embedded in the process-emissions CO2e coefficient.
rollout_limit_notes: Indicative upper-bound envelope only. Does not represent commodity markets, land competition or herd/crop stock dynamics.
availability_conditions: Available in all milestone years as coarse residual sector states; not suitable for detailed agricultural technology competition.
evidence_summary: Anchored to official agriculture energy totals, official agriculture emissions totals, and ABS output-value data, with explicit bundle-allocation assumptions.
derivation_method: Coarse residual-sector synthesis. Official agriculture energy and emissions totals are allocated to two output bundles using transparent assumptions so the MVP model can retain agriculture without process-level build-out.
review_notes: Another coarse residual sector block. Useful for whole-economy emissions and liquid-fuels demand, but not robust for commodity-level agricultural supply analysis.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Split into cattle, sheep, dairy, broadacre crops, horticulture, fertiliser and manure management submodules.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Would likely remain partly reduced-form even in TIMES, but could expand into livestock and cropping activity/process chains with explicit non-CO2 emissions modules.

output_quantity_basis: One million 2024 Australian dollars of farm-gate output in the named coarse agricultural bundle.
cost_components_summary: Placeholder incremental non-energy supply/abatement cost. Full agricultural production cost is outside Phase 1 scope.
input_basis_notes: Direct on-farm energy inputs only, allocated from official agriculture sector totals using bundle-level assumptions. Input coefficients are coarse and value-normalised.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct on-farm energy and process emissions represented at a coarse bundle level. Major non-CO2 agricultural emissions are embedded in the process-emissions CO2e coefficient.
rollout_limit_notes: Indicative upper-bound envelope only. Does not represent commodity markets, land competition or herd/crop stock dynamics.
availability_conditions: Available in all milestone years as coarse residual sector states; not suitable for detailed agricultural technology competition.
evidence_summary: Anchored to official agriculture energy totals, official agriculture emissions totals, and ABS output-value data, with explicit bundle-allocation assumptions.
derivation_method: Coarse residual-sector synthesis. Official agriculture energy and emissions totals are allocated to two output bundles using transparent assumptions so the MVP model can retain agriculture without process-level build-out.
review_notes: Highly simplified mitigation state. Use for coarse sensitivity only until a proper agricultural abatement supply model is built.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Split into cattle, sheep, dairy, broadacre crops, horticulture, fertiliser and manure management submodules.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Would likely remain partly reduced-form even in TIMES, but could expand into livestock and cropping activity/process chains with explicit non-CO2 emissions modules.

state_description: Representative commercial building-service bundle with current electricity, gas and liquid-fuel shares from official energy data.
output_quantity_basis: Baseline-normalised building service bundle. One unit approximates the useful service delivered by one GJ of incumbent 2025 final-energy use in this subsector.
cost_components_summary: Annualised appliance/HVAC/water-heating and other non-energy service-delivery cost excluding explicit commodity purchases. Carbon costs excluded.
input_basis_notes: Direct final-energy inputs only, normalised to the baseline service bundle. Electricity is entered as a final-energy commodity and should pick up grid emissions via the electricity-supply sector.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct on-site emissions only. Electricity-related emissions are excluded from end-use coefficients and should be represented by electricity-supply states to avoid double counting. Biogenic CO2 from biomass combustion is excluded from CO2e unless otherwise noted.
rollout_limit_notes: Indicative upper bound only; no explicit housing-stock rollover or retrofit segmentation in Phase 1.
availability_conditions: Available in all milestone years; interpreted as aggregate service-delivery archetypes rather than individual technologies.
evidence_summary: Baseline fuel shares are anchored to official Australian energy statistics; electrification direction is informed by AEMO and state electrification evidence.
derivation_method: Hybrid. 2025 incumbent coefficients are matched to official sector fuel shares. Prospective states apply electrification and efficiency reductions consistent with Australian electrification studies, smoothed across milestone years.
review_notes: Baseline coefficients reproduce official commercial final-energy fuel shares in 2023–24 when service demand equals 314.2 PJ_service_eq.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Split into space conditioning, water heating, cooking and appliances, with new-build versus retrofit variants.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Each reduced-form state can later map to multiple end-use technologies in a TIMES demand module, potentially using useful-energy service demands and stock-rollover dynamics.

cost_components_summary: Annualised appliance/HVAC/water-heating and other non-energy service-delivery cost excluding explicit commodity purchases. Carbon costs excluded.
input_basis_notes: Direct final-energy inputs only, normalised to the baseline service bundle. Electricity is entered as a final-energy commodity and should pick up grid emissions via the electricity-supply sector.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct on-site emissions only. Electricity-related emissions are excluded from end-use coefficients and should be represented by electricity-supply states to avoid double counting. Biogenic CO2 from biomass combustion is excluded from CO2e unless otherwise noted.
rollout_limit_notes: Indicative upper bound only; no explicit housing-stock rollover or retrofit segmentation in Phase 1.
availability_conditions: Available in all milestone years; interpreted as aggregate service-delivery archetypes rather than individual technologies.
evidence_summary: Baseline fuel shares are anchored to official Australian energy statistics; electrification direction is informed by AEMO and state electrification evidence.
derivation_method: Hybrid. 2025 incumbent coefficients are matched to official sector fuel shares. Prospective states apply electrification and efficiency reductions consistent with Australian electrification studies, smoothed across milestone years.
review_notes: Captures a pragmatic commercial electrification pathway without requiring full end-use disaggregation in Phase 1.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Split into space conditioning, water heating, cooking and appliances, with new-build versus retrofit variants.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Each reduced-form state can later map to multiple end-use technologies in a TIMES demand module, potentially using useful-energy service demands and stock-rollover dynamics.

candidate_expansion_pathway: Split into space conditioning, water heating, cooking and appliances, with new-build versus retrofit variants.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Each reduced-form state can later map to multiple end-use technologies in a TIMES demand module, potentially using useful-energy service demands and stock-rollover dynamics.

state_description: Residential services with strong heat-pump and appliance-efficiency uptake but some residual gas or biomass in the near term.
output_quantity_basis: Baseline-normalised building service bundle. One unit approximates the useful service delivered by one GJ of incumbent 2025 final-energy use in this subsector.
cost_components_summary: Annualised appliance/HVAC/water-heating and other non-energy service-delivery cost excluding explicit commodity purchases. Carbon costs excluded.
input_basis_notes: Direct final-energy inputs only, normalised to the baseline service bundle. Electricity is entered as a final-energy commodity and should pick up grid emissions via the electricity-supply sector.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct on-site emissions only. Electricity-related emissions are excluded from end-use coefficients and should be represented by electricity-supply states to avoid double counting. Biogenic CO2 from biomass combustion is excluded from CO2e unless otherwise noted.
rollout_limit_notes: Indicative upper bound only; no explicit housing-stock rollover or retrofit segmentation in Phase 1.

state_label: Incumbent cement with current clinker ratio
state_description: Reduced-form incumbent Australian cement-equivalent supply with current clinker ratio and recalibrated mixed-fuel kiln use for better national commodity closure.
output_quantity_basis: One tonne of cement-equivalent product at plant gate, represented on a blended cement basis rather than pure clinker.
cost_components_summary: Annualised kiln, grinding and process-equipment cost excluding explicitly listed fuel, electricity and sequestration service purchases. Carbon prices excluded.
input_basis_notes: Calibration-oriented incumbent kiln fuel mix. Coal, gas and biomass shares were re-tuned in the balance-ready pack to improve 2025 commodity closure while preserving the incumbent cement route.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct plant emissions only. Process emissions represent clinker calcination at a cement-equivalent basis using clinker-factor assumptions.
rollout_limit_notes: Indicative upper-bound envelope only. SCM availability, waste-fuel supply and CCS infrastructure constraints are only partly represented.
availability_conditions: Conventional and low-clinker states are available from 2025; CCS ramps gradually from 2030 via the max-share envelope.
evidence_summary: Strongly anchored to Australian CIF decarbonisation pathway evidence, cross-checked with current Australian production and international IEA intensity benchmarks.
derivation_method: Australian industry pathway synthesis. Clinker ratio and clinker process-factor evidence are converted to per-tonne cement-equivalent process emissions, while fuel and electricity coefficients reflect CIF pathway and current industry-energy data.
review_notes: Balance-ready incumbent cement state. Intended for national reduced-form calibration, not plant-level replication.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Split to clinker kiln, SCM supply, cement grinding and CCS modules, with explicit clinker-to-cement material balances.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Can expand to explicit clinker and cement processes in TIMES with separate kiln and grinding technologies plus capture/storage links.
balance_tuning_note: Incumbent kiln fuel mix re-tuned in the balance-ready pack to improve 2025 commodity closure while preserving the conventional cement route.

state_description: Cement-equivalent production with lower clinker factor, higher SCM use and substantially higher alternative-fuel/biomass substitution.
output_quantity_basis: One tonne of cement-equivalent product at plant gate, represented on a blended cement basis rather than pure clinker.
cost_components_summary: Annualised kiln, grinding and process-equipment cost excluding explicitly listed fuel, electricity and sequestration service purchases. Carbon prices excluded.
input_basis_notes: Direct thermal fuels, electricity and sequestration service only. Limestone and minor raw material flows are omitted; process emissions are represented separately.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct plant emissions only. Process emissions represent clinker calcination at a cement-equivalent basis using clinker-factor assumptions.
rollout_limit_notes: Indicative upper-bound envelope only. SCM availability, waste-fuel supply and CCS infrastructure constraints are only partly represented.
availability_conditions: Conventional and low-clinker states are available from 2025; CCS ramps gradually from 2030 via the max-share envelope.
evidence_summary: Strongly anchored to Australian CIF decarbonisation pathway evidence, cross-checked with current Australian production and international IEA intensity benchmarks.
derivation_method: Australian industry pathway synthesis. Clinker ratio and clinker process-factor evidence are converted to per-tonne cement-equivalent process emissions, while fuel and electricity coefficients reflect CIF pathway and current industry-energy data.
review_notes: This is the strongest non-CCS abatement state for Australian cement in Phase 1 because it is directly anchored to CIF pathway ratios and fuel-switch assumptions.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Split to clinker kiln, SCM supply, cement grinding and CCS modules, with explicit clinker-to-cement material balances.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Can expand to explicit clinker and cement processes in TIMES with separate kiln and grinding technologies plus capture/storage links.

emissions_boundary_notes: Direct plant emissions only. Process emissions represent clinker calcination at a cement-equivalent basis using clinker-factor assumptions.
rollout_limit_notes: Indicative upper-bound envelope only. SCM availability, waste-fuel supply and CCS infrastructure constraints are only partly represented.
availability_conditions: Conventional and low-clinker states are available from 2025; CCS ramps gradually from 2030 via the max-share envelope.
evidence_summary: Strongly anchored to Australian CIF decarbonisation pathway evidence, cross-checked with current Australian production and international IEA intensity benchmarks.
derivation_method: Australian industry pathway synthesis. Clinker ratio and clinker process-factor evidence are converted to per-tonne cement-equivalent process emissions, while fuel and electricity coefficients reflect CIF pathway and current industry-energy data.
review_notes: Balance-ready incumbent cement state. Intended for national reduced-form calibration, not plant-level replication.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Split to clinker kiln, SCM supply, cement grinding and CCS modules, with explicit clinker-to-cement material balances.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Can expand to explicit clinker and cement processes in TIMES with separate kiln and grinding technologies plus capture/storage links.
balance_tuning_note: Incumbent kiln fuel mix re-tuned in the balance-ready pack to improve 2025 commodity closure while preserving the conventional cement route.

state_description: National average electricity supply dominated by the incumbent thermal fleet with declining coal share and some gas backup.
output_quantity_basis: One MWh of delivered grid electricity at national-average reduced-form system boundary.
cost_components_summary: Annualised generation, storage, transmission proxy, fixed O&M and balancing/system integration cost. Explicit fossil fuel commodity inputs are listed separately; policy carbon costs are excluded.
input_basis_notes: Indicative direct fuel inputs to the residual thermal portion of the grid-supply state. Non-fuel renewable energy inputs are implicit in the output cost rather than represented as commodities.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct stack and process-equivalent power-sector emissions intensity at the reduced-form electricity-supply boundary; upstream fuel-cycle emissions excluded.
rollout_limit_notes: Phase-1 indicative national upper bound only. Regional power-system constraints are deferred.
availability_conditions: Available in all milestone years; national frontier formulation only in Phase 1.
evidence_summary: Anchored to Australian official generation and emissions context, plus AEMO ISP and CSIRO GenCost system-cost / emissions-intensity benchmarks.
derivation_method: Synthesis. Direct emissions trajectories are calibrated to recent Australian grid intensity and official projection context; output cost follows CSIRO whole-system benchmark points with smoothed milestone interpolation.
review_notes: Intended as the declining incumbent benchmark. In Phase 1 this is a national average, not a state-specific dispatch profile.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Split into state/NEM region supply curves, renewable classes, storage classes, and explicit thermal backup processes.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: This single reduced-form state family can later expand into TIMES supply technologies plus seasonal/time-slice balancing, with cap-act representation for generation and storage.
balance_tuning_note: 2025 electricity intensity nudged to the official benchmark average so that default incumbent generation activity reproduces the national 2025 electricity emissions total.
benchmark_balance_note: Electricity-sector coal and gas inputs are generation fuels, not end-use final energy. Use baseline_electricity_balance.csv for generation-output closure and baseline_commodity_balance.csv for final electricity closure.

input_basis_notes: Indicative direct fuel inputs to the residual thermal portion of the grid-supply state. Non-fuel renewable energy inputs are implicit in the output cost rather than represented as commodities.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct stack and process-equivalent power-sector emissions intensity at the reduced-form electricity-supply boundary; upstream fuel-cycle emissions excluded.
rollout_limit_notes: Phase-1 indicative national upper bound only. Regional power-system constraints are deferred.
availability_conditions: Available in all milestone years; national frontier formulation only in Phase 1.
evidence_summary: Anchored to Australian official generation and emissions context, plus AEMO ISP and CSIRO GenCost system-cost / emissions-intensity benchmarks.
derivation_method: Synthesis. Direct emissions trajectories are calibrated to recent Australian grid intensity and official projection context; output cost follows CSIRO whole-system benchmark points with smoothed milestone interpolation.
review_notes: Represents an efficient but more conservatively firmed near-zero electricity state. Useful as a sensitivity state when the model values residual emissions reduction more highly than system cost.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Split into state/NEM region supply curves, renewable classes, storage classes, and explicit thermal backup processes.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: This single reduced-form state family can later expand into TIMES supply technologies plus seasonal/time-slice balancing, with cap-act representation for generation and storage.
benchmark_balance_note: Electricity-sector coal and gas inputs are generation fuels, not end-use final energy. Use baseline_electricity_balance.csv for generation-output closure and baseline_commodity_balance.csv for final electricity closure.

state_description: Reduced-form incumbent high-temperature industrial heat with a recalibrated national-average mix of coal, natural gas and existing biomass/other industrial bioenergy use.
output_quantity_basis: One GJ of useful industrial heat delivered within the specified temperature band.
cost_components_summary: Annualised heat-supply equipment and non-energy operating cost excluding explicit commodity purchases and carbon costs.
input_basis_notes: Calibration-oriented reduced-form incumbent heat mix. Fuel coefficients were re-tuned in the balance-ready pack to improve 2025 Australian commodity closure while retaining a plausible mixed-fuel incumbent representation.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct on-site emissions only. Electricity-related emissions are excluded from end-use coefficients and should be represented by electricity-supply states to avoid double counting. Biogenic CO2 from biomass combustion is excluded from CO2e unless otherwise noted.
rollout_limit_notes: Indicative upper bound only. No explicit retrofit eligibility, resource competition or process-specific constraints beyond broad plausibility.
availability_conditions: Available in all milestone years as stylised reduced-form heat-service states.
evidence_summary: Built from Australian manufacturing fuel-mix context and planning evidence on industrial efficiency/electrification, with explicit low-confidence caveats.
derivation_method: Stylised synthesis. Temperature-band state families are chosen to preserve a fuel-switch and efficiency response in the MVP model while deferring explicit process modelling.
review_notes: Balance-ready incumbent. Use as a calibration-oriented national average, not a plant-specific archetype.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Replace with subsector/process-specific service or process states (e.g., food boilers, alumina calciners, chemical steam, minerals kilns).
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Could expand to multiple industrial demand technologies or process modules in TIMES, including explicit stock turnover and retrofit/new-build splits.
balance_tuning_note: Fuel mix re-tuned in the balance-ready pack to reduce 2025 coal overstatement and improve national commodity closure. Treat as a calibration-oriented incumbent rather than a literal subsector average.

input_basis_notes: Representative direct heat-supply energy inputs only. Coefficients are deliberately reduced-form and not tied to a single subsector or facility.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct on-site emissions only. Electricity-related emissions are excluded from end-use coefficients and should be represented by electricity-supply states to avoid double counting. Biogenic CO2 from biomass combustion is excluded from CO2e unless otherwise noted.
rollout_limit_notes: Indicative upper bound only. No explicit retrofit eligibility, resource competition or process-specific constraints beyond broad plausibility.
availability_conditions: Available in all milestone years as stylised reduced-form heat-service states.
evidence_summary: Built from Australian manufacturing fuel-mix context and planning evidence on industrial efficiency/electrification, with explicit low-confidence caveats.
derivation_method: Stylised synthesis. Temperature-band state families are chosen to preserve a fuel-switch and efficiency response in the MVP model while deferring explicit process modelling.
review_notes: Useful generic representation for low-temperature industrial heat; not a substitute for food, paper or chemical subsector models.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Replace with subsector/process-specific service or process states (e.g., food boilers, alumina calciners, chemical steam, minerals kilns).
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Could expand to multiple industrial demand technologies or process modules in TIMES, including explicit stock turnover and retrofit/new-build splits.

output_quantity_basis: One GJ of useful industrial heat delivered within the specified temperature band.
cost_components_summary: Annualised heat-supply equipment and non-energy operating cost excluding explicit commodity purchases and carbon costs.
input_basis_notes: Representative direct heat-supply energy inputs only. Coefficients are deliberately reduced-form and not tied to a single subsector or facility.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct on-site emissions only. Electricity-related emissions are excluded from end-use coefficients and should be represented by electricity-supply states to avoid double counting. Biogenic CO2 from biomass combustion is excluded from CO2e unless otherwise noted.
rollout_limit_notes: Indicative upper bound only. No explicit retrofit eligibility, resource competition or process-specific constraints beyond broad plausibility.
availability_conditions: Available in all milestone years as stylised reduced-form heat-service states.
evidence_summary: Built from Australian manufacturing fuel-mix context and planning evidence on industrial efficiency/electrification, with explicit low-confidence caveats.
derivation_method: Stylised synthesis. Temperature-band state families are chosen to preserve a fuel-switch and efficiency response in the MVP model while deferring explicit process modelling.
review_notes: The achievable share remains uncertain and should be stress-tested.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Replace with subsector/process-specific service or process states (e.g., food boilers, alumina calciners, chemical steam, minerals kilns).
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Could expand to multiple industrial demand technologies or process modules in TIMES, including explicit stock turnover and retrofit/new-build splits.

state_description: Exploratory engineered removal state representing direct air capture with permanent storage under large cost uncertainty.
output_quantity_basis: One tonne of CO2-equivalent durably removed from the atmosphere at the reduced-form sector boundary.
cost_components_summary: Representative full removal supply cost, including MRV and permanence/handling proxies. Commodity inputs listed separately where relevant. Policy transfer payments excluded.
input_basis_notes: Direct operational energy inputs only. For land sequestration most cost is implicit land, establishment and MRV cost rather than explicit commodity use.
emissions_boundary_notes: Negative process-emissions coefficient denotes net removal. Positive energy-emissions coefficient captures on-site operational emissions where present.
rollout_limit_notes: Activity cap is an indicative annual upper bound only; land competition, permanence and infrastructure constraints are not fully modelled.
availability_conditions: Land sequestration is available immediately but constrained; DACCS is exploratory and effectively unavailable in material quantities before 2030.
evidence_summary: Removals are treated cautiously, using official Australian projections for land-sink context and international DAC cost evidence for engineered removals.
derivation_method: Exploratory supply-state synthesis. Kept separate from ordinary production sectors so users can enable or disable removals explicitly.
review_notes: Not recommended for core MVP runs without explicit scenario review. Included to preserve an engineered-removal placeholder in the library.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Land-use competition, permanence, reversal risk and MRV modules for biological sinks; explicit DAC process chain and storage network for DACCS.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Would likely map to separate negative-emissions technologies in TIMES with explicit storage links and policy controls.

state_description: Conventional diesel road freight service benchmark, spanning medium and heavy road freight at a national-average level.
output_quantity_basis: One tkm of delivered road transport service at national-average reduced-form boundary.
cost_components_summary: Annualised vehicle/platform, maintenance and non-energy service-delivery cost excluding explicit energy commodity purchases and excluding policy carbon costs.
input_basis_notes: Direct final-energy input only. Electricity and hydrogen take upstream emissions through their own supply sectors if those sectors are modelled.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct on-site emissions only. Electricity-related emissions are excluded from end-use coefficients and should be represented by electricity-supply states to avoid double counting. Biogenic CO2 from biomass combustion is excluded from CO2e unless otherwise noted.
rollout_limit_notes: Indicative national adoption bound only. No explicit vehicle stock turnover or infrastructure optimisation in Phase 1.
availability_conditions: All states are available from 2025 in the reduced-form library, but uptake bounds implicitly reflect infrastructure and stock turnover constraints.
evidence_summary: Service calibration uses official BITRE passenger-km or tonne-km and official Australian road-energy totals; pathway direction uses Australian EV and road-freight studies.
derivation_method: Hybrid synthesis. Incumbent states are calibrated to aggregate Australian road energy and freight/passenger task data; transition states reflect Australian drivetrain efficiency and cost evidence with smoothed milestone trajectories.
review_notes: 2025 coefficient reproduces roughly 36 MtCO2e road-freight emissions when combined with BITRE road tonne-km.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Split by light-duty passenger, buses, vans/light commercial, medium trucks and heavy articulated trucks, with explicit stock rollover.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: These reduced-form service states can map to demand technologies in TIMES with vintaged vehicle stocks, charging/fuelling infrastructure and explicit occupancy/load factors.

cost_components_summary: Annualised vehicle/platform, maintenance and non-energy service-delivery cost excluding explicit energy commodity purchases and excluding policy carbon costs.
input_basis_notes: Direct final-energy input only. Electricity and hydrogen take upstream emissions through their own supply sectors if those sectors are modelled.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct on-site emissions only. Electricity-related emissions are excluded from end-use coefficients and should be represented by electricity-supply states to avoid double counting. Biogenic CO2 from biomass combustion is excluded from CO2e unless otherwise noted.
rollout_limit_notes: Indicative national adoption bound only. No explicit vehicle stock turnover or infrastructure optimisation in Phase 1.
availability_conditions: All states are available from 2025 in the reduced-form library, but uptake bounds implicitly reflect infrastructure and stock turnover constraints.
evidence_summary: Service calibration uses official BITRE passenger-km or tonne-km and official Australian road-energy totals; pathway direction uses Australian EV and road-freight studies.
derivation_method: Hybrid synthesis. Incumbent states are calibrated to aggregate Australian road energy and freight/passenger task data; transition states reflect Australian drivetrain efficiency and cost evidence with smoothed milestone trajectories.
review_notes: Transitional state capturing the abatement that can be obtained without full drivetrain switch.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Split by light-duty passenger, buses, vans/light commercial, medium trucks and heavy articulated trucks, with explicit stock rollover.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: These reduced-form service states can map to demand technologies in TIMES with vintaged vehicle stocks, charging/fuelling infrastructure and explicit occupancy/load factors.

emissions_boundary_notes: Direct on-site emissions only. Electricity-related emissions are excluded from end-use coefficients and should be represented by electricity-supply states to avoid double counting. Biogenic CO2 from biomass combustion is excluded from CO2e unless otherwise noted.
rollout_limit_notes: Indicative national adoption bound only. No explicit vehicle stock turnover or infrastructure optimisation in Phase 1.
availability_conditions: All states are available from 2025 in the reduced-form library, but uptake bounds implicitly reflect infrastructure and stock turnover constraints.
evidence_summary: Service calibration uses official BITRE passenger-km or tonne-km and official Australian road-energy totals; pathway direction uses Australian EV and road-freight studies.
derivation_method: Hybrid synthesis. Incumbent states are calibrated to aggregate Australian road energy and freight/passenger task data; transition states reflect Australian drivetrain efficiency and cost evidence with smoothed milestone trajectories.
review_notes: 2025 coefficient is chosen so that passenger-road and freight-road incumbent states jointly reproduce official road transport energy within about 0.2% when using BITRE service volumes.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Split by light-duty passenger, buses, vans/light commercial, medium trucks and heavy articulated trucks, with explicit stock rollover.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: These reduced-form service states can map to demand technologies in TIMES with vintaged vehicle stocks, charging/fuelling infrastructure and explicit occupancy/load factors.

state_description: Conventional blast-furnace / basic-oxygen-furnace crude steel route using iron ore, coal and some gas, with small scrap input.
output_quantity_basis: One tonne of crude steel at plant gate, reduced-form national average route archetype.
cost_components_summary: Annualised process conversion cost excluding explicitly listed commodity feedstocks, fuels and sequestration service; carbon prices excluded.
input_basis_notes: Direct material and energy inputs only. Auxiliary fluxes and minor alloying materials are omitted in Phase 1.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct on-site route emissions only. Electricity-related emissions are excluded from end-use coefficients and should be represented through electricity-supply states if desired.
rollout_limit_notes: Indicative upper-bound envelope only. Scrap availability, ore quality, hydrogen supply and plant replacement constraints are only partly represented.
availability_conditions: Conventional and scrap routes are available from 2025; CCS and H2 primary routes are treated as material only from 2030 onward through their share caps.
evidence_summary: Built from Australian steel-transition studies plus IEA route benchmarks and industry context, with explicit low-confidence caveats for project-specific future routes.
derivation_method: Route archetype synthesis. Per-tonne energy and process emissions are benchmarked to conventional, CCS-enabled, scrap-EAF and H2-DRI route literature, then smoothed across milestone years.
review_notes: Route intensity is consistent with conventional primary steel benchmarks rather than a plant-specific Australian reconciliation.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Split into explicit BF-coke sinter-hot metal-BOF chain; scrap-EAF chain; DRI shaft furnace + EAF or electric smelter chain; capture, compression and storage modules.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: This sector should ultimately expand to explicit process chains with material balances, capture modules, and possibly capacity vintages.
benchmark_balance_note: For 2025 benchmark commodity closure, BF-BOF coal is mapped 40% to final-energy coal and 60% to process_reductant_coal. Gross model input demand still retains 100% of the coal input for pricing and process representation.

review_notes: Included because CCS remains a plausible transitional primary steel option, but Australian evidence is sparse and route economics are highly project-specific.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Split into explicit BF-coke sinter-hot metal-BOF chain; scrap-EAF chain; DRI shaft furnace + EAF or electric smelter chain; capture, compression and storage modules.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: This sector should ultimately expand to explicit process chains with material balances, capture modules, and possibly capacity vintages.
benchmark_balance_note: For 2025 benchmark commodity closure, BF-BOF coal is mapped 40% to final-energy coal and 60% to process_reductant_coal. Gross model input demand still retains 100% of the coal input for pricing and process representation.

state_description: Near-zero primary steel archetype covering hydrogen direct reduced iron with electric melting/finishing (EAF or electric smelting family).
output_quantity_basis: One tonne of crude steel at plant gate, reduced-form national average route archetype.
cost_components_summary: Annualised process conversion cost excluding explicitly listed commodity feedstocks, fuels and sequestration service; carbon prices excluded.
input_basis_notes: Direct material and energy inputs only. Auxiliary fluxes and minor alloying materials are omitted in Phase 1.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct on-site route emissions only. Electricity-related emissions are excluded from end-use coefficients and should be represented through electricity-supply states if desired.
rollout_limit_notes: Indicative upper-bound envelope only. Scrap availability, ore quality, hydrogen supply and plant replacement constraints are only partly represented.
availability_conditions: Conventional and scrap routes are available from 2025; CCS and H2 primary routes are treated as material only from 2030 onward through their share caps.
